# Twyne

> Twyne is a local-first writing desk with a "room of editors" in residence.
> You file a short brief about what you're writing, then draft while AI
> personas read along, leave marginalia on your sentences, and grade the draft
> against a rubric. Work lives in the browser by default and only syncs when
> you sign in. The AI is bring-your-own-key: the user picks the provider and
> model.

Twyne is for writers who want editorial pushback while they draft, without
handing their work to a hosted service. The default AI provider is Anthropic's
Claude; OpenAI, Google, and self-hosted gateways (Bifrost, Rivet) are also
supported. Authentication supports email + one-time codes, passkeys, and
Bluesky / ATProto sign-in.

## Docs

- [The Manual](https://twyne.love/docs/index.html.md): the full Twyne guide — the dossier interview, the room of editors, the rubric, folios, the apparatus.
- [FAQ](https://twyne.love/faq/index.html.md): common questions about the room, AI, sync, and privacy.
- [Pricing](https://twyne.love/pricing/index.html.md): Twyne's free and Pro tiers.
- [Terms of Service](https://twyne.love/terms/index.html.md): the legal terms for using Twyne.
- [Privacy Policy](https://twyne.love/privacy/index.html.md): what Twyne stores, where it lives, and how to delete it.

## Optional

- [Field Notes (blog)](https://twyne.love/blog/index.html.md): updates, experiments, and editorial notes from the Twyne desk.
- [Download Twyne](https://twyne.love/downloads/index.html.md): the desktop app for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/Diogenesoftoronto/twyne): source code, issues, and contribution guide.
